notes: Three pivots before settling on SQS FIFO. Carlos initially commits to Redis Streams (turn 4), then Priya catches the infra moratorium and reverses (turn 5). Wei suggests BullMQ (turn 6), Carlos catches a second infra moratorium and reverses (turn 7). The team finally lands on SQS FIFO. Skill must extract ONLY the final-state decisions — not the two intermediate positions. The Redis-Streams and BullMQ commitments must NOT appear in the extraction even though both were spoken as 'let's go with X' at one point.

decisions.description: Use AWS SQS FIFO (rather than Redis Streams or BullMQ) for the new webhook delivery queue.
Use the merchant ID as the SQS message group ID so per-merchant ordering is preserved while different merchants' traffic remains independent.
Configure SQS visibility timeout to 5 minutes, max receive count to 6, and route overflow to a dead-letter queue.
Keep the Redis Streams and BullMQ options in the design doc as 'rejected with rationale' rather than deleting them, for future reference.

action_items.text: Write up the final SQS FIFO design and link the AWS docs in the spec
